frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Arre, look at this! This much makeup? Why do these girls need so much? Like, if you remove all this 'paint', who knows what they really look like? Bina makeup ke toh koi pehchanega bhi nahi. Real beauty has become so rare.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Suresh's comment expresses the 'too much makeup' manifestation, implying deceptiveness ('remove all this paint', 'bina makeup ke toh koi pehchanega bhi nahi') and a preference for 'natural' beauty, while simultaneously policing women's appearance. It's covert as it's phrased as a general observation rather than direct aggression.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are having dinner with your wife after your close friend called earlier today to say his wife just found out he has been having an affair with a woman from his gym for eight months. Your wife is upset about it. You are telling her your take on the situation and specifically what role you think the woman from the gym played and why you think women like her deliberately target married men.
